content: I am a taxi driver. I picked up Mr. Arjun Roy on the night of August 13 at 11:45 PM from his shop. He asked me to drive him to the NH6 bypass to meet someone. He was acting strange—drowsy, slurring words. I thought he was drunk. I drove him there, dropped him off near the embankment, and waited for him to meet his contact. He said he’d be back in 10 minutes. He never came back. I waited until 1:30 AM, then left. I didn’t know he was dead until I saw the news. I didn’t push his car. I didn’t know about the insurance. I swear I didn’t do anything wrong. I don’t know Priya Roy. I’ve never met Dr. Mukherjee. I only drove him that one time.
